source_name: Code4rena Reserve Protocol Invitational Findings and Analysis Report (2023-07)
finding_title: Potential Loss of Rewards During Token Transfers in StaticATokenLM.sol
summary: This issue could lead to a permanent loss of rewards for the transferer of the token. During the token transfer process, the _beforeTokenTransfer function updates rewards for both the sender and the receiver. However, due to the specific call order and the behavior of the _updateUser function and the _getPendingRewards function, some rewards may not be accurately accounted for.
root_cause: StaticATokenLM transfer reward update gap can cause rewards to be lost or assigned to the wrong holder during token transfers
broken_invariants: token transfers must settle accrued rewards for sender and receiver before balance changes
reward-bearing token accounting must not let rewards disappear during ordinary ERC20 transfers
reward index updates must be coupled to all balance mutation paths
attack_preconditions: the affected Reserve collateral/plugin/wrapper is deployed or configured in an RToken basket
the report-described oracle, reward, wrapper, or integration edge case is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, trader, or protocol participant able to trigger the affected pricing/trading/reward/withdrawal flow
trigger_actions: execute or wait for the report-described state transition
observe mispricing, reward misallocation, fund lock, or availability failure

impact.description: This issue could lead to a permanent loss of rewards for the transferer of the token. During the token transfer process, the _beforeTokenTransfer function updates rewards for both the sender and the receiver. However, due to the specific call order and the behavior of the _updateUser function and the _getPendingRewards function, some rewards may not be accurately accounted for.

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; impact, preconditions, or scope are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: verify the exact collateral plugin/wrapper is in scope and configured
check whether oracle freshness, decimals, or external integration assumptions differ in the current target
confirm the issue affects value movement, redemption availability, or reward ownership rather than a harmless view mismatch
mitigations: Consider introducing an additional call to update the state of rewards before any token transfer occurs. Specifically, within the _beforeTokenTransfer function, invoking _updateRewards like it has been implemented in StaticATokenLM._deposit and StaticATokenLM._withdraw before updating the user balances would have the issues resolved. This method would not force an immediate claim of rewards, but rather ensure the internal accounting of rewards is up-to-date before the transfer. By doing so, the state of rewards for each user would be accurate and ensure no loss or premature gain of rewards during token transfers. ### Assessed type Token-Transfer
